title: [phase:critique] [family:backend-fit] [provider_hint:codex] Return one concise line with your initial critique of using Google AI agent tooling as the main backend for Cursorvers.Inc. Findings first, one or two sentences only.
summary_text: Highest-risk gaps before calling wrapper integration “complete”: - Tests are missing at the real call site: there are no `line-webhook/index.ts` integration tests for `polish:`/`check:` dispatch and wrapper result handling, so the new wrapper path in [index.ts:735](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:735) and [index.ts:821](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:821) is not directly verified. - Wrapper test suite is not stable in a clean env: running wrapper/orchestrator tests fails due top-level Supabase client init (`supabaseUrl is required`) from [supabase.ts:22](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/supabase.ts:22), pulled via [cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts:1](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-wrapper-facade.ts:1). - CI/docs drift remains: docs still reference non-existent `.github/workflows/test-line-webhook.yml` ([README.md:5](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/README.md:5), [line-webhook test README:73](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/test/README.md:73)), while actual CI is [ci-tests.yml:149](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/.github/workflows/ci-tests.yml:149). - Duplicated production logic still diverges from tested utilities: command parsing is tested in [webhook-utils.ts:83](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/webhook-utils.ts:83) and [webhook-utils.ts:99](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/webhook-utils.ts:99), but runtime uses separate inline parsing in [index.ts:973](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:973) and [index.ts:978](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:978). - Keyword detection is duplicated in two modules ([diagnosis-handlers.ts:44](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/lib/diagnosis-handlers.ts:44), [webhook-utils.ts:45](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/webhook-utils.ts:45)), increasing drift risk. - Contract drift risk remains around optional outputs: wrapper makes `formattedMessage` optional ([cursorvers-wrapper-contracts.ts:31](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-wrapper-contracts.ts:31)), but handler treats missing `formattedMessage` as failure path ([index.ts:822](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/line-webhook/index.ts:822)). - Orchestrator is currently unused by runtime (only tests reference it), so there are now two parallel integration models to maintain ([cursorvers-orchestrator.ts:32](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers_line_free_dev/supabase/functions/_shared/cursorvers-orchestrator.ts:32)). Minimum to finish: add index-level wrapper integration tests, remove/merge duplicate parser/keyword logic, make wrapper tests env-independent, reconcile docs/CI references, and choose one runtime contract path (direct wrapper vs orchestrator).
